root_text.sn47.9:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati veḷuvagāmake.
root_text.sn47.9:1.4: “etha tumhe, bhikkhave, samantā vesāliyā yathāmittaṁ yathāsandiṭṭhaṁ yathāsambhattaṁ vassaṁ upetha.
root_text.sn47.9:1.6: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho te bhikkhū bhagavato paṭissutvā samantā vesāliyā yathāmittaṁ yathāsandiṭṭhaṁ yathāsambhattaṁ vassaṁ upagacchuṁ.
root_text.sn47.9:1.7: Bhagavā pana tattheva veḷuvagāmake vassaṁ upagacchi.
root_text.sn47.9:2.1: Atha kho bhagavato vassūpagatassa kharo ābādho uppajji, bāḷhā vedanā vattanti māraṇantikā.
root_text.sn47.9:2.2: Tatra sudaṁ bhagavā sato sampajāno adhivāsesi avihaññamāno.
root_text.sn47.9:2.4: “na kho me taṁ patirūpaṁ, yohaṁ anāmantetvā upaṭṭhāke anapaloketvā bhikkhusaṅghaṁ parinibbāyeyyaṁ.
root_text.sn47.9:2.5: Yannūnāhaṁ imaṁ ābādhaṁ vīriyena paṭipaṇāmetvā jīvitasaṅkhāraṁ adhiṭṭhāya vihareyyan”ti.
root_text.sn47.9:2.6: Atha kho bhagavā taṁ ābādhaṁ vīriyena paṭipaṇāmetvā jīvitasaṅkhāraṁ adhiṭṭhāya vihāsi.
root_text.sn47.9:2.7: Atha kho bhagavato so ābādho paṭippassambhi.
root_text.sn47.9:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā gilānā vuṭṭhito aciravuṭṭhito gelaññā vihārā nikkhamitvā vihārapacchāyāyaṁ paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.sn47.9:3.2: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn47.9:3.6: Api ca me, bhante, madhurakajāto viya kāyo, disāpi me na pakkhāyanti, dhammāpi maṁ nappaṭibhanti bhagavato gelaññena.
root_text.sn47.9:3.7: Api ca me, bhante, ahosi kācideva assāsamattā:
root_text.sn47.9:3.8: ‘na tāva bhagavā parinibbāyissati, na yāva bhagavā bhikkhusaṅghaṁ ārabbha kiñcideva udāharatī’”ti.
root_text.sn47.9:4.1: “Kiṁ pana dāni, ānanda, bhikkhusaṅgho mayi paccāsīsati?
root_text.sn47.9:4.2: Desito, ānanda, mayā dhammo anantaraṁ abāhiraṁ karitvā.
root_text.sn47.9:4.5: ‘ahaṁ bhikkhusaṅghaṁ pariharissāmī’ti vā, ‘mamuddesiko bhikkhusaṅgho’ti vā, so nūna, ānanda, bhikkhusaṅghaṁ ārabbha kiñcideva udāhareyya.
root_text.sn47.9:4.6: Tathāgatassa kho, ānanda, na evaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn47.9:4.8: Sa kiṁ, ānanda, tathāgato bhikkhusaṅghaṁ ārabbha kiñcideva udāharissati.
root_text.sn47.9:4.9: Etarahi kho panāhaṁ, ānanda, jiṇṇo vuddho mahallako addhagato vayoanuppatto.
root_text.sn47.9:4.12: evameva kho, ānanda, veḷamissakena maññe tathāgatassa kāyo yāpeti.
root_text.sn47.9:5.1: Yasmiṁ, ānanda, samaye tathāgato sabbanimittānaṁ amanasikārā ekaccānaṁ vedanānaṁ nirodhā animittaṁ cetosamādhiṁ upasampajja viharati, phāsutaro, ānanda, tasmiṁ samaye tathāgatassa kāyo hoti.
root_text.sn47.9:5.2: Tasmātihānanda, attadīpā viharatha attasaraṇā anaññasaraṇā, dhammadīpā dhammasaraṇā anaññasaraṇā.
root_text.sn47.9:6.1: Kathañcānanda, bhikkhu attadīpo viharati attasaraṇo anaññasaraṇo, dhammadīpo dhammasaraṇo anaññasaraṇo?
root_text.sn47.9:6.2: Idhānanda, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.9:6.5: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.9:6.6: Evaṁ kho, ānanda, bhikkhu attadīpo viharati attasaraṇo anaññasaraṇo, dhammadīpo dhammasaraṇo anaññasaraṇo.
root_text.sn47.9:6.7: Ye hi keci, ānanda, etarahi vā mamaccaye vā attadīpā viharissanti attasaraṇā anaññasaraṇā, dhammadīpā dhammasaraṇā anaññasaraṇā; tamatagge mete, ānanda, bhikkhū bhavissanti ye keci sikkhākāmā”ti.

translation_text.sn47.9:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the little village of Beluva.
translation_text.sn47.9:1.3: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn47.9:1.4: “Mendicants, please enter the rainy season residence with whatever friends or acquaintances you have around Vesālī.
translation_text.sn47.9:1.5: I’ll commence the rainy season residence right here in the little village of Beluva.”
translation_text.sn47.9:1.6: “Yes, sir,” those mendicants replied. They did as the Buddha said,
translation_text.sn47.9:1.7: while the Buddha commenced the rainy season residence right there in the little village of Beluva.
translation_text.sn47.9:2.1: After the Buddha had commenced the rainy season residence, he fell severely ill, struck by dreadful pains, close to death.
translation_text.sn47.9:2.2: But he endured unbothered, with mindfulness and situational awareness.
translation_text.sn47.9:2.3: Then it occurred to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn47.9:2.4: “It would not be appropriate for me to be fully extinguished before informing my attendants and taking leave of the mendicant Saṅgha.
translation_text.sn47.9:2.5: Why don’t I forcefully suppress this illness, stabilize the life force, and live on?”
translation_text.sn47.9:2.6: So that is what he did.
translation_text.sn47.9:2.7: Then the Buddha’s illness died down.
translation_text.sn47.9:3.1: Soon after the Buddha had recovered from that sickness, he left his dwelling and sat in the shade of the porch on the seat spread out.
translation_text.sn47.9:3.2: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn47.9:3.3: “Sir, it’s fantastic that the Buddha is comfortable,
translation_text.sn47.9:3.6: Because when the Buddha was sick, my body felt like it was drugged. I was disorientated, and the teachings didn’t spring to mind.
translation_text.sn47.9:3.7: Still, at least I was consoled by the thought that
translation_text.sn47.9:3.8: the Buddha won’t be fully extinguished without bringing something up regarding the Saṅgha of mendicants.”
translation_text.sn47.9:4.1: “But what could the mendicant Saṅgha expect from me now, Ānanda?
translation_text.sn47.9:4.2: I’ve taught the Dhamma without making any distinction between secret and public teachings.
translation_text.sn47.9:4.3: The Realized One doesn’t have the closed fist of a tutor when it comes to the teachings.
translation_text.sn47.9:4.5: ‘I shall lead the mendicant Saṅgha,’ or ‘the Saṅgha of mendicants is meant for me,’ let them bring something up regarding the Saṅgha.
translation_text.sn47.9:4.6: But the Realized One doesn’t think like this,
translation_text.sn47.9:4.8: so why should he bring something up regarding the Saṅgha?
translation_text.sn47.9:4.9: Now I am old, elderly and senior. I’m advanced in years and have reached the final stage of life.
translation_text.sn47.9:4.11: Just as a decrepit old cart keeps going by relying on straps,
translation_text.sn47.9:4.12: in the same way, the Realized One’s body keeps going as if it were relying on straps.
translation_text.sn47.9:5.1: Sometimes the Realized One, not focusing on any signs, and with the cessation of certain feelings, enters and remains in the signless immersion of the heart. Only then does the Realized One’s body become more comfortable.
translation_text.sn47.9:5.2: So Ānanda, live as your own island, your own refuge, with no other refuge. Let the teaching be your island and your refuge, with no other refuge.
translation_text.sn47.9:6.1: And how does a mendicant do this?
translation_text.sn47.9:6.2: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.9:6.3: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn47.9:6.5: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.9:6.6: That’s how a mendicant lives as their own island, their own refuge, with no other refuge. That’s how the teaching is their island and their refuge, with no other refuge.
translation_text.sn47.9:6.7: Whether now or after I have passed, any who shall live as their own island, their own refuge, with no other refuge; with the teaching as their island and their refuge, with no other refuge—those mendicants of mine who want to train shall be among the best of the best.”

variant_text.sn47.9:4.11: jajjarasakaṭaṁ → jarasakaṭaṁ (sya-all, pts1ed) | veḷamissakena → veḷumissakena (bj, sya-all, km); vegamissakena (bj); vedhamissakena (pts1ed, mr); vekhamissakena (mr)
variant_text.sn47.9:5.1: phāsutaro → phāsutaraṁ (bahūsu) | tathāgatassa kāyo hoti → tathāgatassa hoti (bahūsu)
